input.layout_description: A simple grassy level that introduces Waddlewings and Super Acorns.
An underground level with tilted pipes and moving crystals. The crystals shift and move as platforms. This level also contains a secret exit.
The first Tower course of the game with shifting platforms and several spinning cogs . Boom Boom is fought here at the end.
This level introduces Yoshis and Monty Moles. There are many hills and rocks; as well as underground caves. Many bushes with Yoshi fruits.
This is an athletic level with many mushroom platforms and Piranha Plants .
This level features slanted grounds, Big Piranha Plants , and Hammer Brothers. Many Piranha Plants roam the area.
A castle filled with lava, swinging platforms held by chains, and Lava Bubbles . Lemmy Koopa is fought here in his airship.
A secret underwater level which can be unlocked by finding the secret exit in Tilted Tunnel . This level introduces Bloopers and unlocks a path to Soda Jungle .
A course with many Waddlewings , Super Acorns , and moving hills.
An underground course with many shifting crystal platforms and Piranha Plants . This level also contains a secret exit .
A tower course with cogs and Burners . Boom Boom is fought here at the end.
A course with fast-moving rolling hills and Yoshis. There are many Yoshi bushes about.
A sky course with many Piranha Plants.
A grassy course with flowers and hedges, one of which is Luigi-shaped, and Big Piranha Plants. Many Piranha plants cover the course.
A dark castle with Munchers and swinging platforms. Lemmy Koopa is fought here in his airship.
An underwater course in which Luigi must evade a Cheep Chomp and huge groups of Cheep Cheeps . This level unlocks a path to Soda Jungle . Full of Cheep Chomps and pipes.
A desert level with shifting Stone-Eyes. Littered with sand geysers, palm trees and Stone-Eyes.
This is a level with Pokeys and Sand Geysers.
It is an underground level with Fire Brothers and Fire Snakes , and introduces Glowing Baby Yoshis .
The tower of Layer-Cake Desert. Tower is full of sliding stone platforms and Grrrols.
A level featuring Spikes and many Flying ? Blocks . This level also contains a secret exit .
The fifth course of Layer-Cake Desert with many Stone Spikes and Mushroom Platforms.
A desert level featuring Lakitus and many Piranha Plants along rolling hills. Many palm trees and rolling hills across the stage.
The castle of Layer-Cake Desert with many shifting platforms that can squash the player. Morton is fought here in his airship .
This level is filled with Fire Piranha Plants and Ice Blocks. They spew fire at the ice blocks, melting them for dangerous paths.
A level featuring many Spikes , similarly to Spike's Spouting Sands from New Super Mario Bros. U . Many Spike's and their spike balls litter the level and pits of quicksand.
A desert cave course with many Pokeys and Grrrols .
A dark blackout cave course with moving platforms, Glowing Baby Yoshis , Fire Brothers , and Big Piranha Plants . The cave is illuminated by the fire and glowing Yoshis.
The second tower of the game. It features many sandy screws, giant sliding platforms, and many opportunities to be crushed. The boss is Boom Boom .
A desert stage featuring moving Stone-Eyes that can push Luigi. This level also contains a secret exit . There are also a few caves with fossils in the walls.
A course with Stone Spikes and moving lifts . Some giant mushrooms and platforms in the sky.
A stage with many spinning hills and Stalking Piranha Plants . There are many rolling hills with small open caves and sand geysers.
A castle with moving blocks that act as bridges to guide Luigi across the lava .
A course featuring slippery land, ladders, and many Piranha Plants . Giant ice chunks with fire spewing Piranha Plants dominate the stage, with a few ladders to dodge them.
This level features several Huckit Crabs and Water Geysers .
An underwater course where many Warp Pipes and Big Urchins are found.
This tower level has many Skewers . The bottom of this tower is flooded with water .
The ruins of a haunted shipwreck infested with Boos . Like Giant Skewer Tower , part of this level is underwater. Part of a giant ghostly sunken ship graveyard .
A course with Yoshi and giant Water Geysers that is full of leaping Cheep Cheeps .
The fourth level of Sparkling Waters includes the two types Urchins and Water Geysers . Many palm trees litter the grassy island level .
This underwater level has Dragoneels , which follows and attacks the player.
This castle contains many grates , burners , and Torpedo Teds . Larry Koopa is fought at the end of this castle. There is a large underwater section where you dodge the Torpedos.
A course which consists of climbing a giant beanstalk. This level's leaf platforms turn brown and fall. This is the only level in the game that features balloon Goombas .
A course with Huckit Crabs and wooden huts. There are water geysers that spray over the wooden platforms.
An underwater course with many Urchins , Big Eep Cheeps , and undertows.
This tower features many Skewers , Fire Bros., and high speed elevators.
The haunted shipwreck level in Sparkling Waters featuring Spinning Boo Buddies and Fish Bones . Takes place in a haunted shipwreck graveyard.
A level where Water Geysers and palm trees are the main footholds. Goombrats and Piranha Plants are in this stage.
A waterfall stage with Water Geysers. Stalking Piranha Plants and leaping Cheep Cheeps make an appearance in this level.
Another underwater level featuring two Dragoneels . There are also Urchins that tumble down from above.
An open castle with King Bills , Torpedo Teds , and Targeting Teds . Larry Koopa is the boss at the end of this castle. There is a large underwater segment where torpedos are fired at the player.
An auto-scrolling level in the forest where Luigi must climb many beanstalks while avoiding Lakitus , Piranha Plants , and Waddlewings . Yoshi makes an appearance in this level.
A snowy level that introduces Munchers and Star Lifts that spin when the player steps on them.
A level that mainly consists of ice and ice pillars and chunks. This level introduces Cooligans that mainly emerge from unenterable Warp Pipes .
The tower of Frosted Glacier with two parts to it. The first part is dodging icicles , while the second one is riding a yellow lift . Boom Boom is the boss of the tower once again.
A frosty level that introduces Prickly Goombas . It is an icy mountain with warp pipes.
A snowy level that includes Scale Lifts and Bullet Bills . This is the only level with Banzai Bills . You climb along the snowy mountain.
An icy cave level with falling icicles, Buzzy Beetles , and Ice Brothers .
A Ghost House where the screen scrolls. The Ghost House is full of Boos.
A castle with moving girders and both types of Thwomps .
A secret course introducing Flipruses , River Piranha Plants , and springboard-like crates.
A course with Star Lifts and Broozers . Takes place in a starry night sky.
A stage where Cooligans slide on Seesaw Shroom platforms.
This tower contains platforms that move under falling icicles . There are many spiky traps above and below.
An ice level featuring Fire Brothers and Fire Piranha Plants . There are open caves with fire melting the ice all throughout the stage.
In this stage, Luigi must use several Scale Lifts to go across large gaps while avoiding Waddlewings .
Luigi can cross the whole level quickly using a Penguin Suit . Takes place in an ice cave, with giant rolling ice hills.
This Ghost House level has floating platforms and Circling Boo Buddies . This level also contains a secret exit .
The Frosted Glacier castle with Big Thwomps . Giant Thwomps crush the ground and destroy platforms.
A secret course featuring many Flipruses . Full of sharp icicles and ice platforms floating in the lake.
The first Airship course in the game with Bowser Jr. as the boss. This level is partly underwater and features Targeting Teds . This is their only appearance in the game.
A course with Big Blocks , Big Goombas , and Big Koopa Troopas . It is full of supersized enemied and blocks.

This course features poison and logs that spin when stepped on. It is a toxic swamp. Several Brick Blocks are found here, and this level also contains a secret exit that goes around Soda Jungle's haunted area.
A course that takes place, as the name implies, in the dark woods with Bramballs . Several Donut Blocks are found here, too.
A tower with Spinners , Amps , Big Amps , and large and normal sized Snake Blocks that scale up the tower .
A Ghost House with multiple doors. Glowing Baby Yoshis are found here, and this level reveals the fact that Baby Yoshis can eat Boos . This level also contains a secret exit .
A course with several unenterable Warp Pipes . This level's background is inspired by Vincent van Gogh 's The Starry Night .
A level featuring a lake where the player must swim through. This level marks the only appearance of Bulbers and Jellybeams in the game. The lake is filled with stationary Stone-Eyes .
A course with poison water and seesaws .
A course with giant and normal sized Wigglers . Takes place over poison water with mushroom platforms.
A castle with giant lava waves with fiery enemies coming from the lava.
A course with giant and normal sized Para-Beetles , along with King Bills . You jump across the Para-Beetles up into the sky; and some colorful blocks.
An airship level with various Hammer Bros . and Mechakoopas .
A stage in which Luigi swings across vines to avoid giant enemies and falling into the poison . There are giant enemies and pipes all throughout the level.
A stage in which block platforms sink and rise as well as appear and retract from the poison water. This level also contains a secret exit .
A course in a mossy jungle area with several Bramballs as obstacles.
A tower stage featuring several Grrrols rolling along regular and giant Snake Blocks to ascend the tower .
A dark Ghost House featuring several Circling Boo Buddies and rotating, swinging, and moving platforms. This level also contains a secret exit .
A painted-style level with several swinging Warp Pipes , some of which are containing Piranha Plants .
An underwater level containing moving Stone-Eyes and navigating around cave ruins.
A stage featuring several moving seesaws and Sumo Bros all over the poison water . The Sumo Bros. shock the bridges.
A vertical-scrolling level in which Luigi must bounce off of Big Wigglers to avoid the rising poison.
A castle featuring swinging chains and waves of lava . You swing along the chains to avoid the lava waves.

A course that primarily focuses on the Para-Beetles and Heavy Para-Beetles . Piranha Plants, Banzai Bills , King Bills , and several P Switches are also present. You must scale the para-beetles to scale to the skies.
This is a clifftop level that features Yoshi , Fruits , and Fuzzies . Fuzzies float above the cliffs to dodge.
Another cliff level over the water , where Porcupuffers attack. Many Urchins can be found, too.
The sixth and final tower where Boom Boom is the boss, the final instance he is fought. The main enemies in this tower are Grrrols .
This level features Chain Chomps , Waddlewings , and red tilting platforms.
A dark cavern filled with giant Light Blocks , Swoopers , Bob-ombs , and ? Switches that light the Light Blocks when activated.
A mountain full of Stalking Piranha Plants , moving lifts , and chains.
A dark cave level with a poison lake and Spine Coasters .
The first and only tower with Sumo Bros . and Boss Sumo Bro . Elastic platforms are found here, and all the Sumo Bros. in this tower are found on them. The platforms are shocked by the Sumo Bros.
This underground level is filled with Cross Lifts , Piranha Plants , Big Piranha Plants , and elastic bars. Full of chances to be crushed by the mechanical cave.
This castle is full of Conveyor Belts and Iron Blocks that can often squish the player.
A rocky cliff level with Fuzzies floating in the clouds with wooden platforms.
An underground level with low water level and a Porcupuffer . There are noteblocks and poles above the water.
A tower full of Brick Blocks destroyable only by Grrrols .
A level with red tilting platforms attacked by Spikes . Wooden cliff structure inhabited by Spikes.
An underground level with self-raising Light Blocks and some fire enemies. The fire lights up the dark.
A level full of Piranha Plants and moving platforms over the clifftop skies.
A level focused on riding Spine Coasters over poison waters in the sky.
A tower level with platform with center cogs and lots of Sumo Bros . You spin up screwtop platforms to ascend the tower.
An underground level with Spike Tops focused on ? Switches chain reactions.
A castle with moving Iron Blocks , Conveyor Belts , and lava .
A level in the skies with moving clouds and quick-moving Brick Blocks .
A level with Seesaw Shrooms , Fuzzies , and Yoshi . You bounce across mushrooms in the sky.
A level above the clouds, filled with arrow lifts and Bullet Bills .
The first and only tower with Magikoopa as the boss. This tower is full of Slide Lifts , as the name suggests.
The last Ghost House in the game where platforms constantly spin. There are also 3 multi-door rooms in this stage. This level also contains a secret exit .
A level set in sunset with Bouncy Clouds and Boomerang Bros. You bounce along the clouds and sky islands to dodge boomerangs.
A cliff-related level with Fuzzies , Water Balls , and Piranha Plants . You climb the mountain through the water bubbles.

output.name: A Quick Dip in the Sky

input.layout_description: Like Bouncy Cloud Boomerangs , this is a sunset cloud level. This level features Paratroopas , Foos , and Snake Blocks .
The Meringue Clouds castle with Ludwig as the boss. This level has Bony Beetles , Big Dry Bones , Fire Bros ., Sledge Bros ., Amps , and several moving platforms moved by gears.

The second and final airship with Bowser Jr. as the boss. This airship has two portions: one on the ground, and another portion on the airship. This is the only level in the game to have Rocky Wrenches and two Checkpoint Flags .
In the first level of the seventh world, Luigi encounters Fuzzies and Ice Bros in the skies.
This level features multiple Munchers and Wigglers .
As its name states, this level is mainly set on halls surrounded by rainbow walls, as well as colorful wooden blocks. Luigi also encounters Bullet Bills and Waddlewings .
The last Tower in the game. Tower full of crushing platforms and girders.
The last Ghost House in the game. Luigi needs to pass platforms that disappear behind him in a spooky house full of Boos. This level also contains a secret exit .
This level features Bouncy Clouds and Boomerang Bros .
This level features multiple rotating platforms, acting as Ferris Wheels. Flame Chomps also appear here.
The Snake Blocks are the main source of footing in the stage aside from other floating platforms. Luigi must avoid Foos that appear here.

Ludwig von Koopa , the last Koopaling to be fought is here. Luigi must be careful on this level to avoid being crushed by the mechancial platforms and gears. Bony Beetles and Fire Bars appear in this castle.
Luigi must be careful to avoid the Mecha Hand on the last airship level in the game. Bowser Jr. is fought here on the giant airship full of cannons and explosives.
This level features moving platforms and Meteors over a lava river.
A level on a raft over a lava lake. Many Monty Moles and other enemies can be found that fall onto the raft, stopping it in place.
A volcano level where rising lava, screw mushrooms and Big Buzzy Beetles are found. You have to ascend the volcano to avoid the lava.
A level where Mario must climb a mountain dodging Meteors . This is Peach's Castle's secret stage.
Here, the player rides and drives a rising elevator which stands still when any enemy or a coin lands on it, while the lava rises. This is the only level in the game that has Bowser Stunners .
The last part of Peach's Castle, where Bowser is fought and Bowser Jr. attacks the player with his Junior Clown Car .
A level that features platforms that rise and sink in the lava, along with falling fireballs and meteors.
A level on a raft over a lava lake. Many Monty Moles and other enemies can be found that fall onto the raft, stopping it in place. Magmaws jump from the lava.
A volcano level that consists of moving cogs, along with Lava Bubbles.
A volcano level with blocks constantly appearing and disappearing and meteors .
A level inside Princess Peach's Castle with rising lava. You dodge stunners on a platform being lifted up, that stops when enemies land on it.
This is the second and last level to have Spine Coasters , preceded by Thrilling Spine Coaster . It is set in a place similar to Acorn Plains . The main enemies here are Fuzzies .
This level has a Layer-Cake Desert setting, where the player must hit P Switches to turn coins into Brick Blocks in order to progress. The player has to keep up pace!
This level takes place in an underwater cavern, where a Cheep Chomp is persistently trying to eat the player. This can be used to defeat enemies and thus open paths.
Taking place in a frozen cave similar to Icicle Caverns , this level features swinging hammer-like platforms that can help the player cross bottomless pits .
Returning to the art style of Painted Swampland , this stage contains poison that can be avoided by riding spinning platforms.
The player must deal with many Huge Fire Bars in this Rock-Candy Mines -like level.
The level has a Meringue Clouds setting, and is populated by Lakitus . The slide lifts from Slide Lift Tower return in this course.
The background is the same as The Final Battle Castle . This course features big-sized spinners and Donut Block floors. Huge spikey balls on chains rotate throughout the halls.
The final level in the game, having a sky setting. Here, the player can use Koopa Shells to get a large amount of extra lives and collect Star Coins . It is a relaxing puzzle stage.
This is the last level where Luigi can find Spine Coasters . It is set in a place similar to Acorn Plains . The main enemies in this level are Fuzzies .
This level has a Layer-Cake Desert setting, where Luigi must hit P Switches in order to progress. Luigi needs to run under falling Iron Blocks while avoiding Spinies and Waddlewings .
Taking place in a haunted shipwreck, Luigi is persistently trying to not be eaten by a Cheep Chomp , who is also useful to defeat enemies.
Taking place in a tower , this level features swinging hammer-like platforms that can help Luigi to climb to the top. Flipruses are also found here.
This level takes place in a level with an identical art style of Painted Pipeworks . This stage contains poison that can be avoided by Brick Blocks which are used as platforms for the level.
Luigi must deal with many Huge Fire Bars in this Rock-Candy Mines -like level. The more Luigi advances in this level, the faster the Huge Fire Bars spin.
In this stage, Luigi must ride Lakitu's Cloud in between many Fuzzies , switching clouds at different points.
A stage where Luigi must dodge many giant spinners . The background is the same as The Final Battle Castle. Giant spikey ball and chains dominate the stage.

The last level of the game. Luigi must use the Flying Squirrel Suit to finish this snow-themed level where many Waddlewings appear. This is the only level of the game where Nabbit is not playable in the single-player mode of the original Wii U version, and the only level where the player can play as a Mii .

A beginning course with grassy fields, Beanpoles, small ponds, and white wooden bridges. There are also Super Bell trees. The Super Bell is introduced in this level. Also has a mega mushroom at the end.
An underground cave course with lamps that players can touch to reveal hidden coins. The stage contains Koopa Troopas and introduces the Fire Flower.
A small fortress arena with two Chargin' Chucks for the players to defeat.
A tall, grassy mountain course with Beanpoles for the players to climb on, and colored blocks similar to those from Super Mario Bros. 3 .
A tropical course containing a large river that can only be crossed with the new creature, Plessie . There are plenty of waterfalls throughout the course as well.
A festival circus tent course with Switch Panels . To open the curtains you must step on all the switch panels.
The first Castle course, with Bowser in his car on a large road over lava . It requires ten Green Stars to unlock, and must be unlocked to progress.
The first Captain Toad's Adventures level, which takes place in a grassy plain and contains Biddybuds and moving platforms.
A desert level with Conkdors . It takes place on high mountainous desert ruins, with high up bridges.
A cosmic level high in the sky with Touchstones . It requires 15 Green Stars to unlock.
An underground level that introduces shadow gameplay and the Potted Piranha Plant .
A grassy level with rolling hills, colored blocks, Galoombas and Skipsqueaks . It also contains a Mario switch that yields a stamp once Mario presses it.
A small rock arena with Big Galoombas surrounded by lava.
A pink grassy level that introduces the Double Cherry and the Blocksteppers .
An auto-scrolling tank level located on a long trail, that also introduces Cannon Boxes and Boom Boom .
The first Mystery House where the player has to beat five Mystery Box levels on time in order to continue.
A snow/ice level with snowballs , Ice Skates , and a Propeller Box . This level introduces the Super Leaf . Lots of ice to skate upon.
An auto-scrolling sky level with moving fences and donut blocks. This level requires 30 Green Stars to unlock. You climb along the fences to avoid falling in the void.
The first Ghost House level that features moving staircases and Boos .
A bright pink grass level with only 100 seconds to reach the Goal Pole . There are clouds and ornate architecture for fences and platforms.
A small fortress arena with three Magikoopas for Mario to defeat.
An underwater level with Cheep Cheeps and Porcupuffers . This is also the second appearance of the Cannon Box . There is a sunken ship at the bottom, and a cute beach at the start and end.
A Super Mario Kart -styled mountain level with Dash Panels and Spring Platforms .
An autumn-themed forest level with Switchboards . There are large paths, mountains and waterfalls to navigate the switchboards through.
A boss level that takes place on a Bowser train. This level introduces Pom Pom .
A circus tent-like boss arena featuring Hisstocrat and his minions.
The second Captain Toad level featuring a water section and a Rammerhead .
A canyon level, where Ant Troopers make their debut.
A forest level with toxic water and many Piranha Creepers. You jump across wooden platforms.
An Enemy Blockade with three Brolders on a small, rock-like platform surrounded by lava.
A technologic-style course located in space with many Beep Blocks .
A grass and athletic level with orange trampolines and hordes of Para-Biddybuds .
A ruins level with Spikes and Galoombas . An ancient desert city full of Spikes and swinging platforms.
The first Fire Bros. Hideout, featuring three of them in a small fortress arena. There are 100 seconds on the timer.
A castle course full of lava. The boss is Boss Brolder . 50 Green Stars are required to open this level.
The second Mystery House containing ten Green Stars .
A beach level with Koopa Troopas , where five Key Coins are required to proceed to a water section with Plessie .
A circus level with trapezes and a Mega Mushroom section.
A very short underground course with blue Lava , Double Cherries , and 100 seconds on the timer. Requires 80 green stars to access. It has a castle-brick design.
An Enemy Battle similar to Chargin' Chuck Blockade , but with five Chargin' Chucks instead of two, while still taking place in the same small fortress arena.
A large savanna-themed mountain with Rabbits and Ant Troopers . Very large and open space with a large mountain at the end.
An underground course with Bob-ombs and many POW Blocks .
A cake-themed course with Red-Blue Panels and the occasional Double Cherry .
A fortress level with spotlights, Hop-Chops , and lots of Cat Bullet Bills . If you get spotted by a spotlight, Bullet Bills launch at you.

The second Fire Bros. Hideout in the game, featuring one of them above a Goomba Tower and two rising stone platforms on top of a much larger and flatter rock-like floor surrounded by lava. There are 100 seconds on the timer.

A Gold Train with a large amount of coins. Accessible by a secret pipe south of King Ka-thunk's Castle. Visible from the northeast corner of World 5. Cannot be played again until 25 more levels are cleared.
A ruins/temple-styled Castle course with Ka-thunks . The boss is King Ka-thunk .
The Captain Toad's Adventures course of World 5. It is a haunted level that features Peepas and a Big Boo .
A mountain-themed level with many Clear Pipes and Fuzzies .
A level taking place on top of multiple haunted ships with many Bullies and Peepas , and an underwater section.
An Oriental-styled level featuring the Goomba Mask . Requires 100 Green Stars to unlock.
A Jungle-themed level with purple, poisonous water and many gates to climb.
A small fortress arena with Clear Pipes, where Prince Bully is fought for the first time.
A snow-themed level with many Ty-foos , spikes and ice skates. You must skate along the ice to avoid their wind gusts.
A mechanical level with many conveyor belts and Bullet Bills . There are some Super Bell Trees dotted about the level too.
A small fortress arena with one Fire Bro along with two Hammer Bros . and two Boomerang Bros . There are 100 seconds on the timer.
A vertical, auto-scrolling mine level where a swarm of Fuzzies chases the player.
The second Tank course, featuring Boom Boom as a boss again.
A circus tent level where players battle against Motley Bossblob .
The third Mystery House challenge, this time with five rooms where the player has to use baseballs.
A blue lava-filled course with Fire Bros and many Clear Pipes .
An underground temple-like course with Switch Panels and Fire Piranha Plants . You light up the areas with fire.
A 100-second lava level with Dash Panels and Donut Blocks . You have to run fast across the platforms.
A course with Bullies and blue lava. There are rising and sinking platforms that follow path patterns.
A blockade featuring Brolders and Boss Brolder on a large rock-like floor surrounded by lava.
A blockade featuring Prince Bully in a small fortress arena. This time he shoots out three blue fireballs at a time and his shoes are blue.
The final Enemy Battle of the game with seven Fire Bros in a small fortress arena. There are 100 seconds on the timer.
A tower surrounded by lava with Thwomps and Fizzlits . It is a delicate climb up avoiding the obstacles.
An underwater course with Rammerheads .
A volcano course with rising and sinking lava with Fire Bros.
A castle course filled with lava featuring the second encounter with Bowser , in his Muscle Car . He is fought on a long road over hot lava. Unlocking this course requires 130 Green Stars .
The Captain Toad's Adventures course of World Castle taking place in a mechanical area with Thwomps and Bullet Bills .
A rainy outdoor fortress course with Spikes and Parabones .
A desert course with Plessie .
A cookie-themed course with spinning cookies.
A train course with Pom Pom as the boss. it travels alongside a carnival in the background.

An outdoor fortress course at night with blue invisible platforms, small sections of which appear when the player stands on them. Bullet bills are launched at the player, which reveal the path when they explode. Thwomps reveal the path when the land.
An underwater fortress course, and the second in World Bowser where Plessie can be ridden, though only in a small segment of the course.
A dark Ghost House with Light Boxes . It also contains a Luigi switch that yields a stamp once Luigi steps on it.
A lava course with several Grumblumps , large crystal blocks with faces; they start slowly rolling when the player walks on them and will go faster when ground-pounded on.
A rematch with a now golden Motley Bossblob , though still taking place in the same circus tent-like arena.
A boss battle with the magenta Hisstocrat taking place in a circus tent-like course.

The final main level in the game where the final boss, Meowser , is fought. The level itself takes place in the carnival area surrounding the very tall tower in the center of it. 170 Green Stars are required to unlock this level.

output.name: The Great Tower of Bowser Land

input.layout_description: The Mystery House of World Bowser; the Cat form is used in all of the sections.
A space course with Skipsqueaks and rainbow-colored pathways.
A space course based on and themed around Super Mario Galaxy . Rosalina is unlocked as a playable character upon first completion of this level.
A wooden-themed athletic course with rolling cylinders. You roll a platform at speed along rails amongst wooden toy blocks.

A sky course with a winged Goal Pole . The pole starts out at the beginning of the level, but it starts flying away so the player must chase it down. There are 100 seconds on the timer and no + Clocks present.
A grassland level filled with giant blocks.
A space course with top-bottom view, featuring "honeycomb" platforms. It follows the top-bottom view of hexagon paths as enemies appear from the sides.
A jungle with Big Galoombas and Mega Mushrooms .
An outdoor snowy Ghost House course with fog, gravestones, disappearing and reappearing ground, and a poison river along which players must safely ride wooden rafts.
A futuristic space course with the Cannon Boxes . 210 Green Stars are required to unlock the level. You take a lean platform along rails, and use a cannon box to switch the paths.
The Captain Toad's Adventures course of World Star, where he can rotate the stage, which itself is mechanical-themed.

A harder version of Really Rolling Hills set at night. The player only gets 30 seconds at the start of the level and must collect extra cyan 10+ clocks. There are spikey enemies and grassy rolling hills across the plains.

output.name: Night Falls on Really Rolling Hills

input.layout_description: A harder version of Mount Beanpole set at sunset. The player must find five Key Coins in order to unlock the Warp Box to the goal pole. This level also introduces the Lucky Bell . Adds more Spike enemies and harder platforming.
A harder version of Deep Jungle Drift that takes place in the dead of night. Because the level is so dark, the players are able to use Light Boxes which emit light rays in their color.

A harder and shorter version of Shadow-Play Alley featuring cannonballs and a Goomba Tower . The Player has only 100 seconds to finish the level, and, there are no time clocks in this level. It takes place in a dungeon entirely with shadows.
A slightly more difficult version of Hands-On Hall set during the day. An Oriental themed stage.
A more difficult version of Spooky Seasick Wreck with Ty-foos and Mega Mushrooms . You jump from tattered ships that are rocking around.
A harder version of Boiling Blue Bully Belt , which requires 240 Green Stars to play. Full of blue fire and lava with moving and sinking platforms.
The Mystery House of World Mushroom featuring the player defeating various enemies to get the Green Stars.
A harder version of Switch Scramble Circus with Fizzlits . You must bounce around and dodge the shocking enemies.
A  harder version of Fuzzy Time Mine with water. Fuzzies chase you up the chasm as you try to escape.
A harder version of Piranha Creeper Creek that takes place in the night. The player has to light up all of the torches in the three areas of this level in order to collect all three Green Stars .
A harder version of Fort Fire Bros. The player starts with only 30 seconds remaining and must collect additional cyan + Clocks across blue lava to gain more time.

A slightly harder version of Sprawling Savanna that takes place in the morning. The course has 500 seconds. It is also the only standard course in the game that is not connected to the previous course by a yellow path, and is available as soon as World Flower -1 is unlocked. Also contains a Toad switch that yields a Green Star once Toad presses it.
A harder version of Shifty Boo Mansion , in a 1940s black-and-white TV aspect and with the exit hidden.
A harder version of Pipeline Lagoon , with Cannon Boxes .
A harder version of Beep Block Skyway . The beep blocks in this level switch four times faster than before. Also contains a Mario switch that yields coins when Mario stands on it.
A harder and shorter version of Sunshine Seaside that takes place at sunset, featuring Goomba Towers with Fire Bros . It has 100 seconds on the timer.
A harder version of Honeycomb Starway set during the day. It is the only level in the game that is a remix of a previous level from a bonus world.
A harder version of Spiky Spike Bridge . This level contains searchlights from Searchlight Sneak , which, if stepped in, cause spikes to appear on the bridges.

As the name suggests, the level is a boss rush where the player must fight six previous bosses. The first five bosses must be fought in order to gain the five keys required to unlock the Warp Box: King Ka-thunk , Pom Pom , Hisstocrat (male and female together), Boss Brolder and Boom Boom . The unlocked Warp Box leads to the sixth and final boss fight: a second rematch with Motley Bossblob , which when defeated leaves a Warp Box which leads to the Goal Pole. All boss fights are now set in space at night.
The final standard course of the game, taking place in space and containing six difficult sections, followed by a congratulations message at the end. It has a slew of difficult challenges in space.
The final Captain Toad's Adventures course, with a big lava pit and a stamp.
The final Mystery House, with 30 Green Stars and a stamp at the end.
A basic grassy level with a Shy Guy and the Toad Brigade up a wooden fort in some simple plains.
A ruins level with Walleyes taking place over a gentle river with grassy banks.
A level with a blue and pink Touchstone that rotate and move when interacted with .
A grassy course with Flaptors and Shy Guys . A stone mushroom sits in the centre, and there are stoney ruins left around.
The first course with a Double Cherry. Is a stone palace with gold onion dome spires and a hedge maze.
A course with many Shy Guys. There is a small grassy hedge maze that leads into a small wooden village, decorated with red banners.
A Ghost House with a Spinwheel that can rotate the center part of the course. There are many bookcases and books you walk across.
A course that features a mine cart. Follows an underground rocky path with a hazardous minecart filled with turnips.
A mechanical course with Bullet Bills. There is a spinwheel with industrial platforms over lava.
A volcanic course where Draggadon is fought. You climb to the top while avoiding Draggadon's fire.
A course with Piranha Creepers . Takes place on the beach with tropical palm trees.
A course mostly underwater. There are a lot of sunken crates, with tropical palm trees on a grassy top.
A jungle course with palm trees and a Turnip Cannon . You fire the cannon at the enemies in the stage.
A Western town populated by Shy Guys with platforms movable by blowing into the microphone. Takes place in a desert / savanna with cacti and dry grass.
An athletic course with Donut Blocks and Dash Pads in the sky.
A course at a Ghost House with many doors and touchstones.
A level set on a snowy train at night. There are star icons on the train.
A dark tower where Wingo is fought. Onion dome spires and stone structures make up the tower.
A level featuring slides and Spinwheels on a grassy plain.
A dark forest course featuring Stumpers. There are many hollowed tree trunks and nailed wooden structures.
A mine cart level in the mountains.
A dark course in a brick town featuring Mud Troopers . There are dead trees and P-Switches.
A grassy water course featuring Goombas in floaties. There are small wooden walkways.
A level featuring toxic water and rafts alongside a rocky jungle.
A brick building featuring Touchstones and bullet bills.
A Ghost House course with Boos and Peepas and many creepy lamps.
A volcanic course with a rematch against Draggadon. Scale to the top of the peak avoiding Draggadon's fireballs.
A course featuring Spinwheels and cogs in desert ruins..
A level taking place on a windup box, with wooden platforms, spikes, and stone walkways.
A level taking place in a white stone flowery gazebo in a grassy plane with a cute hedgemaze.
A large maze of Clear Pipes above a grand city.
A dark forest course featuring Stumpers and Peepas, with iridescent mushrooms and wooden structures.
A palace-like course that makes use of the Double Cherry . There are white spires with purple starry tops with a starry sky.
A fortress-like level featuring Bullet Bills and Touchstones, with industrial plating and blue lava.
A level featuring Spinwheels and Bull's-Eye Bills in the sky on blocky castle structures.
A large level featuring many towers where enemies are fought.
A level featuring Charvaarghs and rolling platforms over lava.
A lava temple featuring Spikes and tilting platforms.
A course featuring the Beep Blocks that change with the beat, and other floating platforms.
A savanna level with Super Pickaxes and Rock Blocks, with a small underwater pool .
A course featuring a huge tree with Ant Troopers. Huge hollowed out trees with a large water geyser.
A snow level where players walk on the right tiles to get the Power Star . They start on the snowy top and go underneath to the mechanical underground.
A cage-level with many Conkdors .
A ruin level in a desert where players need to press P Switches causing stone platforms to rise and fall.
The game's final mine cart level; a grassy ruin level containing Ant Troopers and Mud Troopers, in temple ruins with torches and overgrown vines .
A course featuring Double Cherries and multi-person platforms. There is a large tree and many wooden platforms.
A level featuring many Flip Panels in brick spires in the sky with onion dome roofs.
A large brick fortress with two Turnip Cannons and many Rock Blocks. Takes place in  adesert, with dark orange blocks.
A boss battle against Gold Draggadon inside a golden lava cave. Ascend the peak avoiding the golden fireballs.
A dark temple level with many Shy Guys lit up by torches.
A pinball table over a bright city where the player needs to slide down and activate the five Color Panels at the end to reveal the Power Star.
A level with poison and Double Cherries inside of overgrown jungle temple ruins.
A haunted train level where players must avoid Mud Troopers and dig through Rock Blocks.
A spooky tower level featuring many Magikoopas as you ascend the tower.
A pixelated retro level based on the Donkey Kong arcade game.
A level in the sky with rolling hills .
A Ghost-House level that slowly sinks into poison.
A level featuring an orange and a blue Touchstone .
A large rotating wooden structure in the sky with a Gold Mushroom.
A Japanese-styled building level with Spikes and touchstones .
A space level that rotates when P Switches are pressed filled with Conkdors and Bullet Bills.
A level featuring Dash Panels and lava along a rocky path.
A large industrial tower that sinks into the lava.
The final battle against Wingo at his dark brick Tower.

The first level in New Super Mario Bros. is a meadow with flowers, bushes, and mushrooms scattering the land. Peach's Castle is visible in the background for the first portion of the level. Has a rare Mega Mushroom available in an item box.

This is the second level in the world, and the first underground level which introduces Piranha Plants . It has a red-flag exit that unlocks a different path that directly leads to the tower of that world.
A mushroom level in the hills, and the first sky-themed level. It introduces Spin Blocks and Red Ring . In the level, there are quite a few tilting Mushrooms.
Stone tower which introduces Dry Bones . It is full of moving stone blocks with spikes on them. It has a red-flag exit which unlocks the World 1 - Warp Cannon .
The area contains a Warp Cannon in the middle of the grassland. If the player enters the Warp Cannon, they will be launched to World 5, a snowy world , which can be seen in the background.
This level is a grassy meadow with short grass hills, much like World 1-1. It introduces the Mini Mushroom .
A sky-themed level in this world full of huge mushrooms. This level contains lots of magenta-colored bouncy mushrooms.

This level is the first underwater level in the game. This level introduces Cheep-Cheeps . It is the only secret level in World 1 and the only level where Sushi appear. It has geyser pipes and bouncy bubbles.

This level is the first Castle level in the game, and the last level in World 1. The majority of the level consists of walking on tightropes over lava, and avoiding spikes from the ceiling. This level introduces Podoboos .

A desert-based level with large pyramids in the background. This level introduces Spinies and Pokeys and is the only level where Pokeys appear. There is also quicksand and many floating platforms the Pokeys maneuver around.
The second level of World 2, featuring a pyramid in the background. This level marks the first appearance of Lakitus in the game.

The third level of World 2, the level begins in a desert, with a pipe to the underground. This level introduces the enemy Super Piranha Plant . The level has a red-flag exit , which unlocks the way to World 2-A.

This level is the only bonus level of this world. Although this world is in the middle of a desert, this level is largely inhabited by water. The level has a red-flag exit, which unlocks the way to World 2 - Warp Cannon .

meta.level_code: A Warp Cannon inWorld 1ofNew Super Mario Bros. Wii

input.world: Launches the player character to another area, usually anotherworld
input.layout_description: This area, while not really a level, can be unlocked by using the red-flag exit in World 2-A. The area contains a Warp Cannon in the middle of the desert. If the player enters the Warp Cannon, they are launched to World 5 , which can be faintly seen in the background.

This pipe, only unlockable by completing World 2-A and using the normal exit, allows the player to gain access to the end of World 2. If the player does so, they can skip all levels in the world between 2-4 and World 2 - Castle , where the pipe leads directly to.
The fourth level of World 2, it has a red-flag exit, which unlocks the way to World 2-6, passing a Toad House .
The tower of World 2, the level features many rotating platforms. The boss here, like all other towers, is Bowser Jr.
The sixth level of World 2 after the tower, it introduces Boomerang Bros . and is the only level where Blockhoppers and Fire Snakes appear.
The last level before the castle in World 2, this level is sky-based, filled with yellowish and milky-colored clouds. During most of the level, the player is on a levitating platform.

The last level of World 2, it marks the first appearance of a Spiked Ball in the game. The boss here is Mummipokey . If the player defeats the boss while in their mini form , they will be rewarded with the access to World 4 instead of World 3 .
The first level in World 3 takes place underwater. Cheep-Chomps appear here for the first time in the series and make their only appearance in this game.
This level is the first secret level in World 3. It introduces Skeeters .
This is the second regular level in the world, and mostly takes place on tilting mushrooms. It has a red-flag exit which unlocks a different path that leads to World 3-B .
This is the second secret level in the world. A level high in the sky, it is made entirely out of pipes.
This is the third and final secret level in the world. Completing it leads directly to the world's castle.
This is the third tower in the game. Most of this tower is spent climbing on fences .
This level takes place underwater. It is the only appearance of Bloopers .
This level is the first Ghost House in the game.

meta.level_code: A Warp Cannon inWorld 1ofNew Super Mario Bros. Wii

input.world: Launches the player character to another area, usually anotherworld
input.layout_description: This island and mountain-themed area can be unlocked by using the red-flag exit in World 3 - Ghost House . If the player enters the Warp Cannon , they will be taken to World 6 . To the right of the Warp Cannon is a Roulette Block .
This level is the third castle level in the game, and the last level in World 3. Much of the level consists of avoiding Skewers . At the end, Mario must fight Cheepskipper .
The first level in World 4. This level introduces poison , and the player must use Dorrie to cross it.
This is the second level in the world. It is the only sky level in the world, and mostly takes place on Seesaw Shrooms .
This is the third level in the world, and takes place underwater. It introduces Unagi and Mega Unagi .
This is the fourth tower in the game. Lava is constantly rising upwards.
This is the world's only secret level. It is covered in poison.
This level contains many Wigglers .
This level is the second Ghost House in the game. It contains the franchise's only appearance of Balloon Boos .

meta.level_code: A Warp Cannon inWorld 1ofNew Super Mario Bros. Wii

input.world: Launches the player character to another area, usually anotherworld
input.layout_description: This area can be unlocked by using the red-flag exit in World 4 - Ghost House . If the player enters the Warp Cannon , they will be taken to World 7 , which can be seen in the background.
This level takes place underground. It contains many Bob-ombs .
In this level, Mario must use Dorrie to cross poison again.
This level is the fourth castle level in the game, and the last level in World 4. It contains many swinging platforms and Thwomps .
A level covered in snow featuring Snow Spikes .
An underground level with ice patches , Swoops and Spike Tops .
World 5 has two pairs of pipes, one leads from 5-2 to 5-3, while the other goes from 5-B straight to World 5 - Castle . Both of these are unlocked via red-flag exits .
An aerial level with Stretch Shroom platforms.
A moving platform that scrolls up as Spiked Balls fall on it.
An ice level featuring Snailicorns .
A vertical Ghost House with Broozers .

meta.level_code: A Warp Cannon inWorld 1ofNew Super Mario Bros. Wii

input.world: Launches the player character to another area, usually anotherworld
input.layout_description: A Warp Cannon that takes the player to World 8 , whose dark magenta sky is already visible from this level.
A secret ice level.
A secret underground level featuring Buzzy Beetles and Broozers.
An autoscrolling level with Bullet Bills and Sinking and Rising Mushrooms .
A castle level featuring conveyor belts.
This level introduces thin ledges and has many Bullet Bills .
A secret desert level with Lakitus and the game's only tornadoes .
A beach level containing many Spinies and rising water.
A tower level featuring many Skewers .
A jungle level containing many pipes and Piranha Plants .
A level containing many thin ledges and Fire Bars .
A tower level featuring many conveyor belts .
An underwater level containing the game's only appearance of Deep-Cheeps .
A secret icy mountain level.
A mountainous level containing the game's only appearance of Chain Chomps .
A castle level whose boss is Monty Tank .
A level featuring multiple moving platforms set in the sky.
A Ghost House which contains multiple doors and pathways.
A wrap-around level featuring a controllable rising platform.
A level featuring a giant ridable Wiggler .
A tower level featuring spiked platforms and moving walls.
A level containing multiple mushroom platforms and trampolines .
A grassland level featuring Bob-ombs and a Mega Mushroom .
An athletic level containing multiple mushroom platforms.
A level featuring Donut Blocks and paddle wheels .
A pipe -based level containing the game's only appearance of Squigglers .
A castle level featuring Snake Blocks , whose boss is Lakithunder .
A haunted forest featuring Crowbers and Bullet Bills .
An underground level featuring controllable water and Skeeters .
A tower level featuring moving walls and spikes.
An underwater level featuring Unagi and a Mega Unagi .
An overworld level featuring moving platforms and numerous amounts of Scuttlebugs .
A castle level featuring controllable lifts.
A short level which takes place inside a lava cave.
A wrap-around level taking place inside a volcano , featuring rising lava.
An overworld level with multiple varieties of Hammer Bros .
The last normal level of the game; an overworld level featuring volcanic debris and Kab-ombs .
The last tower level of the game; a tower featuring snake platforms and Fire Bars .
The final level of the game; a large castle level featuring switches that can invert rooms.
Peach's Castle is the first location in the map. The player can buy and watch hint movies here.
The first level of New Super Mario Bros. Wii . It introduces the Propeller Suit , Goombas and Koopa Troopas .
The first underground level of New Super Mario Bros. Wii . It introduces the Ice Flower and Piranha Plants , and is full of moving crystal blocks and Warp Pipes .
This level introduces Yoshi , Venus Fire Traps and the only appearance of Hammer Bros .

meta.level_code: A Warp Cannon inWorld 1ofNew Super Mario Bros. Wii

input.world: Launches the player character to another area, usually anotherworld
input.layout_description: The Warp Cannon of World 1, which leads directly to World 5 . It is unlocked by finding the hidden goal in World 1-3.
The first tower level of New Super Mario Bros. Wii . It has many moving stone blocks and Pendulum Platforms and introduces Dry Bones . The boss is Larry Koopa .
The first underwater level of New Super Mario Bros. Wii . It introduces Cheep Cheeps , Deep-Cheeps , Eep Cheeps , and Clampys .
The first athletic level of New Super Mario Bros. Wii . It contains regular and screw-controlled mushrooms, as well as spinning blocks. This level introduces Koopa Paratroopas .
A grassland level containing many rolling hills. Koopa Troopas and Koopa Paratroopas are the principal enemies.
The first castle level of New Super Mario Bros. Wii . It contains lava pools, Thwomps and giant spinning gears. Its boss is Larry Koopa again.
This level takes place in the desert and is the first level with sand geysers .
This level is filled with barrels and Spikes . There are areas with platforms infested with Koopas and Spikes.
This level is a pitch dark cave, which is dimly lit by lanterns. Fire Bros ., Fire Snakes , Venus Fire Traps and Spike Tops are common enemies in this level.
This tower features metallic fences that the player can cling on. The player will need to avoid spells from Roy coming from above.
Every few seconds, Wind blows in the level, forcing the player to the right.
This level has many Pokeys as well as some Lakitus , and there's also a Yoshi in this level.
This is a mushroom level that mostly has moving coins and blocks.

meta.level_code: A Warp Cannon inWorld 1ofNew Super Mario Bros. Wii

input.world: Launches the player character to another area, usually anotherworld
input.layout_description: The Warp Cannon level of World 2. It shoots the player straight into World 5 .
This castle is filled with lava pits, and three way corridors, of which only one is the correct path. The boss is Roy Koopa .
The level starts in a snowy ground with Cooligans sliding in Mario's direction.
This level marks the first appearance of Ice Bros . and Bullet Bills in the game.
This level is an icy cave level with three parts. There are floating platforms, falling Icicles , and giant falling icicles that Mario can walk on.
This is the first Ghost House in the game. Big Boos , smaller Boos , and possessed furniture are the main obstacles in this level.

meta.level_code: A Warp Cannon inWorld 1ofNew Super Mario Bros. Wii

input.world: Launches the player character to another area, usually anotherworld
input.layout_description: The Warp Cannon level of World 3. It shoots the player straight into World 6 .
This level features brightly colored rotating blocks that must be used to climb to higher locations. This is also the only level to feature Amps .
There are hidden blocks in this level which will turn into solid red blocks when the ! Switch is activated further in the game.
This is a vertical level, and will only autoscroll when all players step on the rotating pink block.
Lemmy Koopa 's castle is riddled with giant falling icicles and frozen floors making timed jumps difficult. The player must move on Ice Snake Blocks throughout the level.
A level introducing Big Urchins that is mostly in water.
Most of this course is filled with Donut Blocks and Cheep Cheeps that will jump up to attack the player.
This is the only level that features Huckit Crabs . There are also many Urchins and Big Urchins.
This tower features many conveyor belts . Iron Block are also a common sight here, being used as both platforms and obstacles.

meta.level_code: A Warp Cannon inWorld 1ofNew Super Mario Bros. Wii

input.world: Launches the player character to another area, usually anotherworld
input.layout_description: The cannon level of World 4. It takes the player straight to World 6 .
This is the only underwater level of World 4.
This is the Ghost House of World 4. Its hidden goal is a shortcut to World 4-Castle .
A beach level with Yoshi and Lakitus .
This is the castle level of World 4 that has the only appearance of fences and Climbing Koopas .
This is the first airship level of the game. Bowser Jr. is fought here.
This level takes place on a series of cliffs populated by Stone Spikes and Bullet Bills.
A level taking place in a Piranha Plant -infested cavern, with many Rolling Hills throughout.
A sewer-themed level mainly featuring Super Piranha Plants , Stalking Piranha Plants , and Buzzy Beetles .
This level mainly takes place on high semisolid cliffs, which Mario and Yoshi can maneuver. This level has the first and only appearance of Monty Moles .
The primary feature of World 6's Fortress is two giant Skewers on the floor and ceiling, which both collide in the middle of the Fort.
This island-themed level primarily features Porcupuffers jumping out of the water. Some large ? Blocks also make an appearance later on.
This dimly-lit cavern level has Mario ride a raft, with floodlights that can be rotated with the Wii Remote. Common enemies here include Mega Cheep-Cheeps and Swoops .

meta.level_code: A Warp Cannon inWorld 1ofNew Super Mario Bros. Wii

input.world: Launches the player character to another area, usually anotherworld
input.layout_description: The cannon level of World 6. The cannon will shoot straight to World 8 .
World 6's Castle primarily features spiked balls and Giant Spiked Balls on pendulum platforms.

World 6's airship features Mechakoopas and has the first and only appearance of Rocky Wrenches . Bowser Jr.'s battle primarily features the introduction (and only appearance) of the Remote-Controlled Clown Car .
This level features volcanic ash and volcanic debris .
This level contains more falling debris and spinning wheels on the ground. This level also contains a secret exit .
This level features giant lava waves that will instantly defeat the player.
This tower is the first level to feature Fire Bars .
This level takes place in an underwater cave, and features Jellybeams , Bulbers , and Stone Spikes .
This level is an auto-scrolling level, featuring Tilt Lifts that can be moved by rotating the Wii Remote .
This level takes place in a volcano with rising lava.
This level is a secret level, and can only be unlocked by taking the secret path on World 8-2.
This is the last airship level of the game.
This is the final castle level of the game and is home to Bowser .
The first level of World 9 is sky-themed and has the athletic background from World 1 . There are spinning rectangle platforms throughout the level.
This stage takes place in the beach setting from World 4 . The player must get to the goal while avoiding a Porcupuffer and many Koopa Troopas .
This course has the background of World 6 . It features a heavy arsenal Missile Bills and Bull's-Eye Banzais .
This course takes place over the Desert Theme of World 2 . It mainly revolves around both Bob-ombs and Venus Fire Traps .
This is an icy course based on World 3 , where the player must get to the top of the Icy Mountain while avoiding Cooligans , Fuzzies , and Big Fuzzies .
This volcanic course is based on World 8 and contains numerous platforms which emerge from and descend into lava .
This course, based on World 5 , takes place in a snowy forest, with floors made of ice blocks containing Munchers and Warp Pipes containing Venus Fire Traps .
The last course of the game is an auto-scrolling sky level, akin to World 7 , which features King Bills as well as Goombas and Fuzzies.
The first Rainbow Course in the game.
A very basic level filled with common enemies like Goombas and Koopa Troopas .
The first underground level in the game, and introduces red POW Blocks and the Mega Mushroom .
A grassy level littered with blocks containing the Super Leaf .
A tower filled with Snake Blocks and undead enemies such as Bone Goombas . The boss is two Reznors on one wheel.
The first Warp Cannon course in the game, which features a dashing mechanic.
The first aerial/sky level in the game and introduces the Gold Ring .
The first underwater level in the game, filled with Cheep Cheeps and the occasional Spiny Cheep Cheep .
A pipe-filled maze with many Piranha Plants and Koopa Troopas.
The first castle level in the game, filled with ropes and Lava Bubbles . The boss is Roy Koopa .
It is a sandy level filled with many moving Brick Block platforms and grounded Piranha Plants .
It is a level high in the sky, littered with gold-colored platforms. It also features Hammer Bros ., Boomerang Bros ., and Sledge Bros .
A simple desert level mostly taking place underground with many Pokeys .
This level is an aerial, ruin-like level filled with Fuzzies that attempt to hinder Mario's progress.
It is a generic tower level with a few Bone Goombas and Dry Bones . The bosses are four Reznor on one wheel .
It is the first Ghost House in the game, and also marks the first appearance of the monstrous Boohemoth .
It is a level filled with Pokeys , Spinies , and Fire Snakes . It also has many blocks that stack up to form pyramid-like structures.
It is a level with many sand blocks and also introduces Chain Chomps .
This level is filled with Para-bombs parachuting from the sky and from cannons.
The level is spent almost entirely on a platform on lava that only moves if the player is stepping on it. The boss is Iggy Koopa .
The Rainbow Course .
This level takes place in the sky with many colored block platforms.
It is a water-themed autoscroll level packed with many Urchins and Deep Cheeps .
This level is a maze-like Ghost House where almost every door leads to a different room, thus comprising of several areas.
This is an ice-themed level taking place underground. Common enemies here are Dry Bones and spiked balls .
It is an underground-themed level cramped with sandy-looking objects, Buzzy Beetles , and red POW Blocks .
The Warp Cannon course of this world. It takes the player to World Flower. It also has a level theme not seen in any other course.
This level is reminiscent of World 1 and is an autoscroll level composed of many Beanstalks and Koopa Troopas .
It is a level full of Whomps that attempt to crush Mario, along with giant spiked balls that destroy everything in their path.
The Rainbow Course , which features coins that rain down from above.
The level is mainly made up of hills of sand. Cheep Cheeps that jump out of the water first appear here.
This level is an underwater level in a beach area.
A cobweb-filled jungle level that also features Scuttlebugs .
An underwater level infested with Urchins.
An autoscrolling, submerged tower with many Spiked Pillars and moving walls.
The Warp Cannon course of this world, which leads to World Flower .
This level is a jungle-themed level with many Wigglers and vines.
This level is a Ghost House that has multiple paths and is also infested with Peepas and Boos .
This level is an underwater level filled with massive boulders that attempt to crush Mario, along with Bloopers hiding in pipes.
This level marks the first appearance of the Mini Mushroom and prominently features it, for the level must be completed with it.
This level is a water-themed castle cramped with dangerous Grinders that attempt to hinder Mario.
The Rainbow Course , featuring coins that drop from the sky and leap from below.
A snowy level featuring chunks of ice and Goombas .
An autoscrolling Ghost House level controlled by Boos that control walls on both sides of Mario.
An icy level with several Koopa Troopas and Piranha Plants scattered throughout.
This level contains many Mushroom Trampolines and Goomba-releasing Warp Pipes .
A level containing rising ice platforms that Mario must maneuver while avoiding enemies such Bone Piranha Plants and Buzzy Beetles .
This level takes place in an icy cave, where Amps can be found following circular tracks .
An icy level containing many slopes and Piranha Plants.
A level that takes place in the sky, where Balance Lifts and tilting mushrooms are found.
A cave level that features Fuzzies and POW Blocks .
An underwater level containing Cheep Chomps .
World 4's Castle level contains many spiked balls and Snake Blocks . Morton Koopa Jr. is the boss of this level.
The Rainbow Course , featuring spinning bars of coins.
This level takes place in the mountains during sunset, and the floor is mainly made up of pipes, mainly infested with Piranha Plants .
This level takes place underground, in an icy area.
In this level, Mario needs to navigate through a rather difficult course of parachuting Para-bombs and rotating and stationary electric Amps , while preventing falling down the large abyss.
A Ghost House with Peepas and a Boohemoth .
This level takes place in a forest, where toxic waters can be found with a Porcupuffer swimming in it trying to attack Mario.
This level takes place in the sky, although a large proportion of the level is land.
This is an aerial level with many bouncy mushroom platforms and Lakitus .
This castle is made up of gray bouncing mushroom platforms and conveyor belts. The boss is Lemmy Koopa .
A level that takes place mostly on colored mushroom blocks in the sky.
This level contains a lot of Lakitus , where the player must use these enemies to their advantage.
A level featuring masses of blocks forming a platform that rocks back and forth.
This place includes lots of slopes, and Mario can use Koopa Shells and Gold Rings to take advantage of the level's rather peculiar architecture.
This Tower level's primary feature is a moving fence that moves with rising lava, along with Burners .
This level uses rising and sinking mushroom platforms a lot.
An auto-scrolling level that mostly takes place on a golden ship flying through the sky.
This level has several Bullet Bills , and it also includes Snake Blocks .
This mountain level features a lot of rickshaw-like lifts. It is the only mountain level of the world despite it being the setting on the map. The Piranha Plant is the most common enemy in the level.
This Castle level centers around fences, including a unique type of fence that can be changed to move up or down the wire using or or .
The Rainbow Course , featuring coins that spin in circles and Mushroom Trampolines .
A volcano level that contains volcanic debris and POW Blocks .
This level is a maze that involves many ? Switches , Warp Doors , and moving platforms.
This level takes place in a volcano, where Mario must ride segmented platforms while avoiding enemies such as Goombas and Para-bombs .
This level contains platforms that change direction whenever he jumps on it. Mario must ride these platforms while avoiding Bullet Bills and Flame Chomps .
An underground level containing Fire Bros and Stretch Shrooms .
This level takes place in a volcano containing raising lava, platforms that sink when Mario touches them, and Dry Bones .
The Tower of World 6. It has several Burners , some of which are blocks that temporarily deactivate the Burners when hit. Reznors are the bosses of the tower.
A haunted level that contains many Crowbers and Lifts carried by Peepas .
This level features intermittent Seesaws , along with spiked balls and Bowser sculptures .
The final level in the main game. This level contains many Burners, along with the Koopalings , who can turn Mario to stone by flashing the Koopa Clown Car 's eyes. Bowser is the boss of the level.
The World Star Rainbow Course .
This level takes place at night. It consists of many Brick Blocks which if the player has a Gold Flower can become a lot of coins .
This level takes place high in the sky, on top of what is supposed to be a deserted castle.
This rather short level's theme is a combination of a beach and Ghost House theme.
This is an auto-scrolling beach-themed level.
This level combines the elements of grass and sky.
Most of this level takes place in an underwater fortress.
The level is a volcano level and is very similar to World 6-1 .
The final Castle of the game. Dry Bowser is fought here.
